channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Liam Stack and Max Kim

item.title: Airstrikes hit Tehran’s Mehrabad Airport.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from multiple explosions near Mehrabad Airport in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: State Department Bypasses Congress to Send Israel More Than 20,000 Bombs
item.description: The sale had been under informal review in Congress, but the State Department declared an emergency because of the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Multiple explosions near Mehrabad airport amid US-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Friday as part of their joint war on Iran.

item.title: Iran’s Navy Is Weakened but U.S. Still Faces Challenges in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. strikes have heavily targeted Iran’s large ships and bases, but its Revolutionary Guard navy is a more nimble force.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Port of Bandar Abbas in the Strait of Hormuz in 2023.

item.title: This is what happened on March 6.
Trump Maintains That U.S. Has Ample Weapons as Iran War Depletes Stocks
item.description: After meeting with defense contractors at the White House, President Trump said the companies were all committed to increasing production.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Farah Stockman and Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said an increase in weapons production would come “as rapidly as possible” to “the highest levels of quantity.”

item.title: Trump Echoes F.D.R. and Grant in Calling for ‘Unconditional Surrender’
item.description: President Trump demanded that Iran capitulate in the war with the United States, invoking a phrase made famous by statesmen and generals.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump declared on Friday that he would settle for nothing short of “unconditional surrender” by Iran.

item.title: Can we say senior UN official? he is the head of an agency
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sahar Al Attar/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz is a major maritime corridor for energy, food and other goods.

item.title: How the War in Iran Is Disrupting the World’s Oil
item.description: Our business reporter Peter Eavis looks at the global implications of the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway to the south of Iran. He analyzes ship activity in the strait, comparing it before and after the United States and Israel initiated attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Sutton Raphael, Leila Medina, Stephanie Swart, Blacki Migliozzi, Christiaan Triebert, Keith Collins, Jacqueline Gu and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How We Analyzed the Strike on the Iranian School
item.description: Malachy Browne of our Visual Investigations team describes what satellite imagery and other evidence tell us about who might be responsible for an airstrike on an elementary school in southern Iran. The strike killed at least 175 people, according to health officials and Iranian state media.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne, Aaron Boxerman, Coleman Lowndes and Estelle Caswell

item.title: Russia Is Sharing Intelligence With Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The information has included satellite imagery showing the locations of military personnel. But some officials played down the significance of the partnership.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia has spoken to his Iranian counterpart to discuss the U.S.-Israel attack.

item.title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
item.description: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Explosions over west Tehran on Friday evening.

item.title: Iran Attacks and Mideast Flights: How Safe Is Air Travel?
item.description: Drone and missile attacks have caused high anxiety across the region, but experts say the danger to commercial airliners is “fairly remote.”
item.dc:creator: Gabe Castro-Root
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Emirates airliner evacuating foreigners from Dubai arrived in Frankfurt on Tuesday.

item.title: Macron speaks with Lebanon’s leader, as France seeks to broker a truce between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Natan Odenheimer and Catherine Porter
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rises after strikes on Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: Israel says it struck an underground bunker at Khamenei’s compound.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Wood, Sanjana Varghese, Ronen Bergman and Euan Ward

item.title: MSC Steps Up to Return Cruise Passengers Stranded in Dubai
item.description: Thousands of guests on at least six ships await evacuation in the Gulf region. With government help slow, MSC, the world’s third-largest line, took matters into its own hands.
item.dc:creator: Ceylan Yeğinsu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The cruise ships Aroya Manara and MSC Euribia anchored at the port of Dubai on March 4. All together, there were about 15,000 cruise passengers stuck in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Families Forced to Sleep on the Streets of Beirut as Israel Pounds Hezbollah
item.description: Thousands fled the southern outskirts of the Lebanese capital before Israel unleashed heavy strikes on the militant group overnight. Many say they have nowhere to go.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shefgar Othman is staying in a tent by Beirut’s seaside promenade with his family after they were told to evacuate their home in the city’s southern suburbs earlier this week.

item.title: Airspace in the region remains heavily restricted.
item.dc:creator: Lazaro Gamio and Zach Levitt

item.title: Israel Says It Struck Underground Bunker at Khamenei’s Compound
item.description: The Israeli military said the facility, under the site where Iran’s supreme leader was killed by a strike last week, was still being used after the first attack.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Ronen Bergman and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: New England Patriots’ Plane Ferried Americans Back From Middle East
item.description: The flight was arranged and paid for by the State Department, officials said, as part of the evacuations of citizens stranded by attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather and Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kirby Lee/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect
item.media:description: A New England Patriots private plane in San Jose, Calif., last month. One of the team’s planes, operated by a charter company, was used to bring Americans home from the Middle East this week.

item.title: Trump Demands ‘Unconditional Surrender’ by Iran, Shifting U.S. Objectives Again
item.description: President Trump laid out yet another, more ambitious goal of U.S. military action, one that could extend the war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians mourning the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader, in Tehran on Thursday night.

item.title: With Iran, Trump Takes the U.S. to War Without the Public’s Support
item.description: In opening a military campaign against Iran, President Trump is the first president in modern times to take the United States to war without the backing of the public.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump on the USS George H.W. Bush in October. Polling shows public support for striking Iran is below what his predecessors enjoyed initially when they used force overseas.

item.title: From 1978: Golda Meir, 80, Dies in Jerusalem; Israelis Acclaim ‘Stalwart Lioness’
item.description: Once a teacher in Milwaukee, she climbed the ranks of the Labor Party to become Israel’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Paul Hoffman and Special to The New York Times

item.title: Iranians flee to Turkey. ‘I am in constant fear,’ says one.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Ali Ihsan Ozturk/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Border crossings between Turkey and Iran have seen a steady stream of Iranian nationals fleeing the American and Israeli strikes in their country.

item.title: Israel issues an evacuation warning in the Qom region, near an Iranian nuclear site.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Fordo nuclear facility in Iran after it was struck by the United States in June.

item.title: Strikes Batter Iran’s Storied Azadi Stadium Complex
item.description: Several sports centers have been hit in Tehran since the U.S. and Israel began their attacks, the authorities say, but none with the resonance of the Azadi.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Azadi Indoor Complex in Tehran was struck on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Remarks on U.S.S. Cole and Iran Raise Questions in Guantánamo Case
item.description: Prosecutors have offered up no evidence supporting a link that the president claimed between Iran and the attack in Yemen in 2000, defense lawyers say.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The bombing of the U.S.S. Cole off the coast of Yemen was seen as a precursor of the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.

item.title: Iran’s Drone Attacks Plague the Gulf States
item.description: Missile launches from Iran have slowed, but relentless drone volleys could deplete air defenses in the region.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damage at an oil facility in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Tuesday after the authorities said it had been hit by debris from an intercepted drone.

item.title: Air travel remains disrupted across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.media:description: Empty check-in counters previously occupied by Emirates and Etihad Airways at John F. Kennedy International Airport, in New York City on Monday.

item.title: This is what happened on March 5.
European stock indexes fall again, ending a turbulent week for markets.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:description: A gas station in Burgos, Spain, on Thursday. A surge in oil and gas prices driven by the Middle East conflict has led to a chaotic week in European stock markets.

item.title: 4 Arrested in London on Suspicion of Spying on Jews for Iran
item.description: The police said one Iranian and three British-Iranians were detained as part of a counterterrorism investigation.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle and Lizzie Dearden
item.media:description: The headquarters of the Metropolitan Police in London. The arrests were part of a long-running investigation, police said.

item.title: Khamenei’s Killing Sparks Anger and Grief in South Asia’s Shiite Muslims
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, was deeply respected among the millions of Shiite Muslims in Pakistan and India.
item.dc:creator: Showkat Nanda, Zia ur-Rehman, Suhasini Raj and Max Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Shiite Muslims during a candlelit protest in Srinagar on Wednesday.

item.title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
item.description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Hezbollah supporters gathering in the southern suburb of Beirut, Lebanon, on Sunday to mourn the death of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: 3 Australians Were on U.S. Submarine That Sank an Iranian Warship
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s disclosure about the attack in the Indian Ocean prompted concern that Australia’s close military cooperation with the United States could draw it into the fighting in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An injured Iranian sailor is moved on a stretcher at Galle National Hospital in Sri Lanka after the torpedoing of the Iranian naval ship IRIS Dena on Thursday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Liam Stack, David E. Sanger and Emmett Lindner

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia went to a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier in Moscow last month.

item.title: Israel Pounds Southern Beirut, a Hezbollah Stronghold
item.description: The strikes were the most intense bombardment since a cease-fire deal in late 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza, Euan Ward, Hwaida Saad and Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Smoke rising after an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, on early Friday.

item.title: State Department Is Accused of Slow Assistance in Iran War for Americans Abroad
item.description: Many veteran diplomats faulted the State Department for its response after the attacks in Iran began, and for its actions beforehand.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Passengers evacuated from the Middle East arrived at Dulles International Airport on Thursday.

item.title: Two Schools in Iran Damaged in U.S.-Israeli Bombing
item.description: Photos show that both schools, southeast of Tehran, sustained damage. No injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Thursday.

item.title: Iran delays naming a new leader out of security concerns, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, participates in the annual Quds Day rally in Tehran, Iran, in 2019.

item.title: Israel Begins Assault in Southern Beirut, Raising Fears of Wider Incursion
item.description: As the war with Iran threatens to engulf more countries in the region, President Trump said he wanted to be involved in picking a new leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Aurelien Breeden, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising after an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, late Thursday and early Friday local time.

item.title: House Turns Back Bid to Restrict Trump’s War Powers in Iran
item.description: A handful of Democrats joined Republicans to defeat an effort to force President Trump to go to Congress for approval to continue using force against Iran, while two G.O.P. lawmakers backed it.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky was one of two Republicans to back the effort.

item.title: Why Torpedoed Iranian Warship Is a Problem for India
item.description: An Iranian ship had been invited by India to take part in peacetime naval exercises with dozens of other countries, days before it was sunk in a U.S. submarine attack.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli and Suhasini Raj
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, in the Bay of Bengal during an international fleet review at Visakhapatnam, India, on Feb. 18.

item.title: Analysis Suggests School Was Hit Amid U.S. Strikes on Iranian Naval Base
item.description: The Feb. 28 school strike in Minab, which killed dozens, including children, appears to have been part of an attack on an adjacent naval base in southern Iran, where officials said U.S. forces were operating.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Planet Labs, Rafaela Balster/The New York Times
